config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dianggap nomor dua hanya karena ada alasan “kepentingan masyarakat”, karena itu mudah disalahgunakan untuk membenarkan penindasan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan menghormati hak dasar setiap orang, bukan dengan mengorbankannya seenaknya. Saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan yang ketat: masyarakat kuat justru ketika individu dilindungi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to excuse abuse by governments and majorities. Public safety and the common good matter, but they should justify only limited, clearly defined restrictions on rights, not a blank check to override them. The strongest societies are usually the ones that protect individual liberty first and then make collective demands only when they are truly necessary and narrowly tailored. If we let “society” become an all-purpose argument, it becomes too easy to silence unpopular people and call it progress.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar, padahal bisa berujung pada pembungkaman, perampasan hak, atau pengabaian kelompok kecil yang tidak punya suara. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas dan perlindungan hukum yang kuat, bukan dijadikan alasan serba boleh. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi individu sambil mencari solusi bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From an American perspective, I am wary of any argument that gives “the common good” enough power to override rights, because that has repeatedly been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. Public safety and shared obligations are real, but they need strict limits, clear rules, and due process rather than vague appeals to society. A healthy society is one that protects individual rights first and only restricts them in narrow, necessary ways.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh secara umum didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai longgar untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, penggusuran, atau menekan kelompok kecil yang suaranya lemah. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, aturan yang jelas, dan proses hukum yang adil, bukan dijadikan alasan serba boleh. Justru masyarakat yang kuat adalah masyarakat yang mampu melindungi individu sekaligus mencari solusi kolektif tanpa mengorbankan martabat orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my American perspective, “the common good” is too often used as a vague excuse for censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when unpopular people have no real power to push back. Public safety and shared obligations matter, but they should come with strict limits, due process, and narrow justification, not a blank check to override rights. I would rather live in a society that sometimes has to work harder to solve problems than one that makes it easier by sacrificing individual liberty.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
